description:
You are working as a junior data-analyst who has just received two quarterly sales
    CSV files located in

        /home/user/datasets/sales_q1.csv
        /home/user/datasets/sales_q2.csv

    Management only wants the rows that satisfy BOTH of the following textual
    conditions:

      • The “Region” column is exactly East (case-sensitive).  
      • The “Product” column begins with the literal text “Widget”
        (again case-sensitive, e.g. “Widget A”, “Widget Pro”, etc.).

    Your assignment:

    1. Using regular-expression based filtering (e.g. grep, awk, sed),
       extract every row that meets the two conditions from BOTH source
       CSV files.

    2. Write all matching rows, preceded by the original header line
       (exactly once), into a new CSV file

           /home/user/output/east_widget_sales.csv

       Output format requirements that will be automatically tested:
         • The very first line MUST be the unaltered header taken from the
           source files.
         • Each subsequent line must be a matching row, fields separated
           by the original commas.
         • The rows must be sorted in ascending date order (yyyy-mm-dd as
           found in the first column).
         • There must be exactly one LF character (Unix newline “\n”)
           after every line, including the final one.

    3. Create a plain-text summary log at

           /home/user/output/extraction.log

       with exactly the following three lines (and nothing else),
       terminated by a single LF each:

           <SOURCE_1_NAME>: <count> matching rows
           <SOURCE_2_NAME>: <count> matching rows
           TOTAL: <total> matching rows extracted

       where <SOURCE_1_NAME> and <SOURCE_2_NAME> are the literal file
       names sales_q1.csv and sales_q2.csv, and the counts reflect the
       number of rows you actually copied into the consolidated CSV from
       each file.  Make sure the numbers match the content of
       east_widget_sales.csv or the automated checker will fail.

    4. Place the finished files in /home/user/output/ (create the
       directory if it does not already exist).

    Only standard POSIX shell utilities are available.  You do NOT have
    root privileges.  Do NOT hard-code the row counts; they must be
    derived by your commands so that the solution would still work if the
    data changed.

    When you are done, the automated tests will look only at:

        /home/user/output/east_widget_sales.csv
        /home/user/output/extraction.log

    Therefore, be absolutely certain that:

      • Both files exist at those exact paths.
      • Their content exactly matches the format described above.
      • File and directory permissions allow read access to everyone
        (default umask is fine).

    No additional console output is required for grading, but feel free
    to print intermediate results while you work.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing data files that the automated grader will provide before the
    agent starts:

        Path: /home/user/datasets/sales_q1.csv
        Contents (including trailing LF on every line):

            Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price
            2023-01-05,East,Widget A,120,9.50
            2023-01-07,West,Widget B,200,9.50
            2023-02-11,East,Thingamajig,75,15.00
            2023-03-02,East,Widget Pro,60,12.00
            2023-03-09,South,Widget A,110,9.50

        Path: /home/user/datasets/sales_q2.csv
        Contents:

            Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price
            2023-04-15,East,Widget A,90,9.50
            2023-05-03,North,Gadget,150,8.00
            2023-05-22,East,Widget Plus,50,11.00
            2023-06-12,East,Thingamajig,40,15.00
            2023-06-25,West,Widget A,130,9.50

    Expected artefacts that MUST exist after the agent finishes:

        1. /home/user/output/east_widget_sales.csv
           Expected contents:

               Date,Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price
               2023-01-05,East,Widget A,120,9.50
               2023-03-02,East,Widget Pro,60,12.00
               2023-04-15,East,Widget A,90,9.50
               2023-05-22,East,Widget Plus,50,11.00

           (Exactly 5 lines, each terminated by \n.)

        2. /home/user/output/extraction.log
           Expected contents:

               sales_q1.csv: 2 matching rows
               sales_q2.csv: 2 matching rows
               TOTAL: 4 matching rows extracted

           (Exactly 3 lines, each terminated by \n.)

    These exact byte sequences will be used for automated verification.
    -->
